domains: From Molecules to Organisms: Structures and Processes

nodes.title: Research on a problem should be carried out before beginning to design a solu...
nodes.description: Research on a problem should be carried out before beginning to design a solution. Testing a solution involves investigating how well it performs under a range of likely conditions.

academic_standards.description: Research on a problem should be carried out before beginning to design a solution. Testing a solution involves investigating how well it performs under a range of likely conditions.

nodes.title: Possible solutions to a problem are limited by available materials and resour...
nodes.description: Possible solutions to a problem are limited by available materials and resources (constraints). The success of a designed solution is determined by considering the desired features of a solution (criteria). Different proposals for solutions can be compared on the basis of how well each one meets the specified criteria for success or how well each takes the constraints into account.

academic_standards.description: Possible solutions to a problem are limited by available materials and resources (constraints). The success of a designed solution is determined by considering the desired features of a solution (criteria). Different proposals for solutions can be compared on the basis of how well each one meets the specified criteria for success or how well each takes the constraints into account.

nodes.title: Human activities in agriculture, industry, and everyday life have had major e...
nodes.description: Human activities in agriculture, industry, and everyday life have had major effects on the land, vegetation, streams, ocean, air, and even outer space. But individuals and communities are doing things to help protect Earth's resources and environments.

academic_standards.description: Human activities in agriculture, industry, and everyday life have had major effects on the land, vegetation, streams, ocean, air, and even outer space. But individuals and communities are doing things to help protect Earth's resources and environments.

nodes.title: Nearly all of Earth's available water is in the ocean
nodes.description: Nearly all of Earth's available water is in the ocean. Most fresh water is in glaciers or underground; only a tiny fraction is in streams, lakes, wetlands, and the atmosphere.

academic_standards.description: Nearly all of Earth's available water is in the ocean. Most fresh water is in glaciers or underground; only a tiny fraction is in streams, lakes, wetlands, and the atmosphere.

nodes.title: Earth's major systems are the geosphere (solid and molten rock, soil, and sed...
nodes.description: Earth's major systems are the geosphere (solid and molten rock, soil, and sediments), the hydrosphere (water and ice), the atmosphere (air), and the biosphere (living things, including humans). These systems interact in multiple ways to affect Earth's surface materials and processes. The ocean supports a variety of ecosystems and organisms, shapes landforms, and influences climate. Winds and clouds in the atmosphere interact with the landforms to determine patterns of weather.

academic_standards.description: Earth's major systems are the geosphere (solid and molten rock, soil, and sediments), the hydrosphere (water and ice), the atmosphere (air), and the biosphere (living things, including humans). These systems interact in multiple ways to affect Earth's surface materials and processes. The ocean supports a variety of ecosystems and organisms, shapes landforms, and influences climate. Winds and clouds in the atmosphere interact with the landforms to determine patterns of weather.

nodes.title: The orbits of Earth around the sun and of the moon around Earth, together wit...
nodes.description: The orbits of Earth around the sun and of the moon around Earth, together with the rotation of Earth about an axis between its North and South poles, cause observable patterns. These include day and night; daily changes in the length and direction of shadows; and different positions of the sun, moon, and stars at different times of the day, month, and year.

academic_standards.description: The orbits of Earth around the sun and of the moon around Earth, together with the rotation of Earth about an axis between its North and South poles, cause observable patterns. These include day and night; daily changes in the length and direction of shadows; and different positions of the sun, moon, and stars at different times of the day, month, and year.

nodes.title: The sun is a star that appears larger and brighter than other stars because i...
nodes.description: The sun is a star that appears larger and brighter than other stars because it is closer. Stars range greatly in their distance from Earth.

academic_standards.description: The sun is a star that appears larger and brighter than other stars because it is closer. Stars range greatly in their distance from Earth.

nodes.title: Matter cycles between the air and soil and among plants, animals, and microbe...
nodes.description: Matter cycles between the air and soil and among plants, animals, and microbes as these organisms live and die. Organisms obtain gases, and water, from the environment, and release waste matter (gas, liquid, or solid) back into the environment.

academic_standards.description: Matter cycles between the air and soil and among plants, animals, and microbes as these organisms live and die. Organisms obtain gases, and water, from the environment, and release waste matter (gas, liquid, or solid) back into the environment.

nodes.title: The food of almost any kind of animal can be traced back to plants
nodes.description: The food of almost any kind of animal can be traced back to plants. Organisms are related in food webs in which some animals eat plants for food and other animals eat the animals that eat plants. Some organisms, such as fungi and bacteria, break down dead organisms (both plants or plants parts and animals) and therefore operate as "decomposers." Decomposition eventually restores (recycles) some materials back to the soil. Organisms can survive only in environments in which their particular needs are met. A healthy ecosystem is one in which multiple species of different types are each able to meet their needs in a relatively stable web of life. Newly introduced species can damage the balance of an ecosystem.

academic_standards.description: The food of almost any kind of animal can be traced back to plants. Organisms are related in food webs in which some animals eat plants for food and other animals eat the animals that eat plants. Some organisms, such as fungi and bacteria, break down dead organisms (both plants or plants parts and animals) and therefore operate as "decomposers." Decomposition eventually restores (recycles) some materials back to the soil. Organisms can survive only in environments in which their particular needs are met. A healthy ecosystem is one in which multiple species of different types are each able to meet their needs in a relatively stable web of life. Newly introduced species can damage the balance of an ecosystem.

nodes.title: Plants acquire their material for growth chiefly from air and water
nodes.description: Plants acquire their material for growth chiefly from air and water.

academic_standards.description: Plants acquire their material for growth chiefly from air and water.

nodes.title: Food provides animals with the materials they need for body repair and growth...
nodes.description: Food provides animals with the materials they need for body repair and growth and the energy they need to maintain body warmth and for motion.

academic_standards.description: Food provides animals with the materials they need for body repair and growth and the energy they need to maintain body warmth and for motion.

nodes.title: The energy released [from] food was once energy from the sun that was capture...
nodes.description: The energy released [from] food was once energy from the sun that was captured by plants in the chemical process that forms plant matter (from air and water).

academic_standards.description: The energy released [from] food was once energy from the sun that was captured by plants in the chemical process that forms plant matter (from air and water).

nodes.title: The gravitational force of Earth acting on an object near Earth's surface pul...
nodes.description: The gravitational force of Earth acting on an object near Earth's surface pulls that object toward the planet's center.

academic_standards.description: The gravitational force of Earth acting on an object near Earth's surface pulls that object toward the planet's center.

nodes.title: No matter what reaction or change in properties occurs, the total weight of t...
nodes.description: No matter what reaction or change in properties occurs, the total weight of the substances does not change. (Boundary: Mass and weight are not distinguished at this grade level.)

academic_standards.description: No matter what reaction or change in properties occurs, the total weight of the substances does not change. (Boundary: Mass and weight are not distinguished at this grade level.)

nodes.title: Measurements of a variety of properties can be used to identify materials
nodes.description: Measurements of a variety of properties can be used to identify materials. (Boundary: At this grade level, mass and weight are not distinguished, and no attempt is made to define the unseen particles or explain the atomic-scale mechanism of evaporation and condensation.)

nodes.title: The amount (weight) of matter is conserved when it changes form, even in tran...
nodes.description: The amount (weight) of matter is conserved when it changes form, even in transitions in which it seems to vanish.

nodes.title: Matter of any type can be subdivided into particles that are too small to see...
nodes.description: Matter of any type can be subdivided into particles that are too small to see, but even then the matter still exists and can be detected by other means. A model showing that gases are made from matter particles that are too small to see and are moving freely around in space can explain many observations, including the inflation and shape of a balloon and the effects of air on larger particles or objects.

academic_standards.description: Matter of any type can be subdivided into particles that are too small to see, but even then the matter still exists and can be detected by other means. A model showing that gases are made from matter particles that are too small to see and are moving freely around in space can explain many observations, including the inflation and shape of a balloon and the effects of air on larger particles or objects.
